opp.ais_why:
• The November 13 2025 demo (63 minutes) with Julio Lozano and six Foundever stakeholders is the strongest piece of evidence. Julio's reaction is captured as "Very, very pleasant surprise" at the feature set, and Andreina called the demo "very thorough and very interesting". The buyer engaged on AI-driven negotiation parameters, asking transparency questions on AI discount recommendations.
• A captured pain point reads "Need transparency on AI-driven discount recommendations to avoid overpaying". That is the buyer actively evaluating AI behaviour, not just being pitched. Products include Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake; AI importance is 'Important'; and the RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management was responded to on 3/31.
• Multiple Foundever attendees - Antonino, Julio, Mariana, Andreina, David, Sadoc and Ana - joined the AI demo and asked detailed AI-related questions on multi-parameter AI negotiation, smart onboarding agent preview, and autonomous negotiation with market benchmarks. Strong cross-functional buyer engagement on AI-specific capabilities underpins the Hungry call.

sources: Avoma S2P Demonstration pt.2 2025-11-05 (Julio, Sadoc Medina)
metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated a clear cost-driven rationale but no fully quantified business case yet. Julio Lozano framed it bluntly on the 2026-03-05 call: 'It's gonna be the most optimized cost solution that we can try get at the very end' and used a worked example ('if we are paying now $100 for the full suite of Coupa, and we are paying $25... we are not interested in paying on top'), signalling they want to pay only for the 3-4 modules they need, not a full P2P suite. On the 2025-11-05 sourcing demo, Julio quantified currency-consolidation value ('20 millions of Philippine pesos... it's just a matter of $200,000'), and Sadoc Medina stressed savings tracking ('savings is very important... total spend... $5,000,000 opportunity'). The hard number still missing is Zycus's own modular pricing — which the buyer needs to build the ExecComm business case. Quantifying ROI vs Coupa's renewal cost is the open move.

champion.narrative: Julio Lozano (Global Category Manager IT) is the developing champion: he drives the selection committee, sourced Zycus into the RFP, proactively brokers internal alignment, and owns the eSourcing use case as a primary user. What is missing is proven access to and advocacy with the economic buyer (CFO/ExecComm). Critically, the rep has under-served this champion: the pricing he needs to advocate upward has been outstanding since 2025-12-03. Equip Julio with assumption-based modular pricing plus a one-page business-case deck he can carry to the CFO — that is what converts him from developing to strong.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent and live competitive reference: used today for sourcing, contracting (metadata only), onboarding and transactional P2P, now in a 2-year ramp-down negotiation. Julio noted 2026-03-05 that Coupa is 'not happy to see we are in a wrap up' and is pushing the full bundle, conflicting with Foundever's reduced, cost-optimized scope. That commercial friction is Zycus's wedge: the buyer explicitly wants to pay only for the 3-4 modules S/4 does not cover, and Coupa's bundle-only posture is misaligned. Other unnamed RFP respondents are in the down-select. Risk: if Coupa concedes an acceptable modular ramp-down, incumbent familiarity makes them the 'safe' option — which is exactly why getting our priced, integration-validated case in front of the committee FIRST matters.

sources: Avoma S2P Demonstration pt.2 2025-11-05 (Sadoc Medina, Ana Vides)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clear and operationally specific. Foundever is migrating all 42 countries onto SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud; S/4 covers only transactional P2P, leaving eSourcing, vendor onboarding, vendor management and contracting uncovered, and the company is exiting Coupa (Julio 2026-03-05: 'company decision is we need to move away from the Coupa platform'). They run dual approval flows and an e-sourcing tool that 'requires an IT ticket' to add an evaluator and lacks bulk question creation. They cannot do true CLM — Coupa is 'a repository and a holder of the metadata' only. Global multi-currency/multi-language gaps for LATAM/APAC/Africa suppliers recur. Pain owners: Julio (eSourcing) and Sadoc (sourcing functionality), with Ana/Andreina on global reach.

sources: Avoma S2P Demonstration 2025-11-13 (Julio Lozano, mutual NDA)
SF Next Step (Coupa renewal EOY, Jan 2027 go-live)
paper_process.narrative: Paper mechanics partly visible. Julio committed on 2025-11-13 to send a mutual NDA — the first paper step, still open. The hard contracting forcing function is the Coupa contract expiry at EOY 2026 and the Jan 2027 US/Philippines go-live; a signed solution is needed well before then. The bi-directional S/4HANA integration scope must be defined before an SOW can be finalized. No MSA/SOW redlining, legal, or InfoSec review has occurred, and no paper owner is named on our side. With a Jan 2027 go-live looming, naming a paper owner and pre-staging MSA/SOW + InfoSec sequencing should follow the shortlist decision.

economic_buyer.narrative: The true economic buyer is the Executive Committee / Global CFO, who is not yet engaged. Julio Lozano explained the structure on 2026-03-05: 'we don't have a global head of procurement... for now it's a team on the procurement leadership' and 'our global CFO is supervising us in his very few minutes.' Julio is the lead evaluator and process driver but NOT the signer; final award requires an ExecComm business-case approval. Because this is a champion-driven tender, the path to the CFO runs THROUGH Julio — arm him to broker it; do not cold-outreach the CFO. Building that path is the highest-value gap after pricing.

decision_process.narrative: Formal RFP run by Foundever procurement leadership. RFP issued ~18 Mar 2026, responses due 31 Mar 2026. Per 2026-04-08 Next Step, Antonino Cappuccio and Julio did initial review evaluating strategy alignment; as of 2026-06-02 still down-selecting to a shortlist. Approval chain: procurement leadership shortlists, an initial licensing+implementation cost estimate goes to ExecComm for business-case approval, then proposals refined April-June to award 'one or two partners.' Committee is widening across NA/LATAM/APAC. NOTE for motion fit: buyer silence during down-select is process, not a slip — do not chase for status; shape the evaluation criteria around our proven edge (modular fit, S/4 integration, AI).

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well-documented from the RFP and three demos. The functional bar is multi-currency, multi-language global sourcing (Ana Vides 2025-11-05: 'could you demonstrate examples outside of The US... Latam or Asia Pacific or Africa'), AI-driven Q&A/translation for non-US suppliers, ease of configuration and supplier self-onboarding (Sadoc Medina 2025-11-05: new supplier setup in 'just one minute'), bulk event creation, auctions, savings/spend analytics, and full contract lifecycle management beyond the metadata-repository use they have today. The overriding decision driver is cost-optimized modular fit (Julio 2026-03-05) and bi-directional SAP S/4HANA integration.

items.detail: August 2026 close date is 56 days away, but buyer is still in shortlist review (as of 2026-06-02). Remaining milestones—shortlist decision, commercial negotiation, ExecComm business case approval, MSA/SOW contracting, implementation prep—realistically require 6+ months, pushing closure to Q4 2026 or later. January 2027 US/Philippines go-live (7 months from today) is the hard forcing function; any delay in vendor selection compresses implementation timeline and raises execution risk.

No CFO or Executive Committee engagement despite ExecComm approval being a required gate for final vendor award (Next Step 2026-03-18: 'presenting a Business Case to the relevant stakeholder approval obtained, refining proposals during April to June for awarding one or two Partners'). Julio Lozano is an Evaluator, not Economic Buyer (per contact role), and has not escalated Zycus to budget authority. The Global CFO is named but has never engaged with Zycus (zero calls, zero Salesforce activities, not on any Avoma attendee list). Without CFO validation of cost case and strategic fit before the ExecComm gate, the deal can stall at commercial review even after shortlist selection. Best Case forecast is indefensible without EB engagement.

Modular pricing (eSourcing, CLM, Vendor Mgmt, Onboarding only) requested by Julio on 2025-12-03, metrics to be provided by buyer, but no pricing delivered yet by Zycus (Marc committed to send metrics questionnaire 'this afternoon or first thing in the morning' on 2025-12-03, now overdue ~6 months). Buyer cannot build business case or compare vs. Coupa without Zycus pricing. This is a blocking deliverable.

Bi-directional SAP S/4HANA integration (vendor master validation and creation, requisition-to-award data flow) is a hard requirement for January 2027 go-live per Julio (2026-03-05 call: 'the onboarding process must validate what we have in S/4 vendor master, and when finished, send all that information and create the register in S/4'). Marc stated '1,100+ APIs, able to integrate transactionally' (2026-03-05 call), but no technical deep-dive, integration design session, or SAP-Zycus architecture review has occurred to validate Marc's claim, define data-flow design, or surface InfoSec/data-governance requirements. Without a validated integration plan, the January 2027 go-live timeline is at risk and Foundever may default to Coupa as the lower-risk incumbent (Coupa already integrated, vs. new-vendor integration unknowns).

Coupa is the incumbent in contract renewal negotiation and has user familiarity, existing integration, and lower implementation risk advantage. Per Julio (2026-03-05 call), Foundever proposed a 2-year modular ramp-down to Coupa, but 'Coupa is not happy... only offering the bundle' (full-suite or nothing). If Coupa agrees to modular pricing at acceptable cost, they remain a strong finalist—Julio stated: 'We are assuming Coupa will be willing to extend the service for the next two years accepting the ramp-down. We are in that hope.' This is a defensive posture: Foundever prefers Coupa modular ramp-down (lowest-risk path) and is running the RFP as the fallback. Zycus must prove superior fit (modular pricing + S/4 integration + AI innovation) AND faster implementation to overcome incumbent bias. If Coupa capitulates, Zycus is fighting an uphill battle. Current Coupa threat level: HIGH.

August 2026 close date is 52 days away, but buyer is still in shortlist review (as of 2026-06-02 Next Step: 'still reviewing submissions for down selection to shortlist'). Remaining milestones—shortlist decision, commercial negotiation, Executive Committee business case approval, MSA/SOW contracting, implementation prep—realistically require 4-6+ months (mid-September to early November 2026), pushing closure to Q4 2026 or later. January 2027 US/Philippines go-live (7 months from today, 2026-06-30) is the hard forcing function driving urgency, but the forecast close date is 8-12 weeks too early for the realistic milestone path. If shortlist decision slips beyond mid-July, August close is impossible.

Modular pricing (eSourcing, CLM, Vendor Mgmt, Onboarding only—no full-suite P2P) requested by Julio on 2025-12-03. Marc committed to send metrics questionnaire 'this afternoon or first thing in the morning' (2025-12-03 call), now overdue 6+ months (as of 2026-06-30). Buyer cannot build business case for Executive Committee approval or compare Zycus vs. Coupa without pricing. This is a blocking deliverable: every week without pricing is a week Foundever advances the RFP shortlist based on incomplete data or defaults to Coupa (incumbent with known cost). Highest-priority vulnerability.

Bi-directional SAP S/4HANA integration is a hard go-live requirement but no technical deep-dive or integration design session has occurred.

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, multi-threaded enterprise S2P tender with real two-way buyer engagement — but the 21 Aug 2026 close (52 days out) is not credible: the buyer is still down-selecting submissions as of 2 Jun 2026 with ExecComm approval, modular pricing, and contracting all still ahead. The deal is live; the date is wrong.

flags: Escalate to CFO and Executive Committee now. The business case must go to ExecComm for final approval (per Next Step 3/18/26), yet no CFO or ExecComm sponsor has joined a call or been introduced. Julio is an engaged Evaluator but does not control budget. The ranked move: Marc and the deal owner's manager co-present a quantified business case (Zycus modular licensing cost + implementation vs. Coupa bundle renewal savings, ROI, and risk mitigation) to Julio for him to champion internally to CFO and ExecComm. Without Economic Buyer validation, Best Case forecast is indefensible and the shortlist can stall at commercial gate.

Activate the AI/Merlin wedge as the strategic differentiator. Foundever's pain (42 countries, multi-language, LATAM/APAC supplier participation gaps, cost pressure) maps perfectly to Zycus Merlin strengths (autonomous negotiation, live translation, multi-currency AI-driven sourcing). Yet the AI story has not been elevated to a business-case pillar. The move: co-author a one-page 'AI-driven procurement at global scale' value prop with Julio, quantifying time savings (autonomous event creation, bulk templates, 3-field supplier onboarding) and cost avoidance (higher supplier participation → better pricing, reduced manual translation effort), and position it as the Zycus differentiator in the ExecComm business case vs. Coupa's static, US-centric bundle.

Competitive displacement strategy: Coupa is vulnerable on bundled-only licensing and US-centricity, but Zycus has not yet run a formal competitive review with the relevant stakeholder move: schedule a 30-minute 'Zycus vs. Coupa: modular fit and global scale' session with Julio and Antonino Cappuccio (EMEA lead, co-reviewer per Next Step 4/8/26) to walk through side-by-side proof points (cost, LATAM/APAC demo evidence from Nov 2025 calls, AI negotiation, SAP integration depth). Arm Julio with a competitive battle card he can use internally to position Zycus as the recommended vendor in the shortlist business case.

Validate S/4 bi-directional integration in a joint technical deep-dive within 2 weeks. Julio's requirement (2026-03-05 call): 'validate vendor master in S/4, send onboarding data and create the register in S/4' is a complex two-way sync. Marc stated 'we have 1,100+ APIs, able to integrate transactionally,' but no integration design session, data-flow architecture review, or SAP-Zycus technical validation has occurred. Without a validated integration plan, the January 2027 go-live timeline is at risk and Foundever may default to Coupa (incumbent integration = lower risk). Action: schedule a 90-minute technical deep-dive with Julio, Antonino (Regional Head Procurement EMEA), David Avila (Digital Finance Sr Mgr), Zycus integration architects, and ideally the SAP S/4 implementation partner to walk through the vendor-master validation and onboarding-to-S/4 data flow, surface InfoSec/data-governance requirements, and document the integration plan as an RFP response addendum. This de-risks Zycus vs. Coupa and creates joint ownership momentum.

Pricing is the gating blocker: the modular vs full-suite pricing Julio asked for on 2025-12-03 is still undelivered ~7 months later, so the buyer cannot build the ExecComm business case or beat Coupa on cost. Delivering it is the single highest-leverage win move.

hard.ais_why:
• The November 13 2025 demo (63 minutes) with Julio Lozano and six Foundever stakeholders is the strongest piece of evidence. Julio's reaction is captured as "Very, very pleasant surprise" at the feature set, and Andreina called the demo "very thorough and very interesting". The buyer engaged on AI-driven negotiation parameters, asking transparency questions on AI discount recommendations.
• A captured pain point reads "Need transparency on AI-driven discount recommendations to avoid overpaying". That is the buyer actively evaluating AI behaviour, not just being pitched. Products include Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake; AI importance is 'Important'; and the RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management was responded to on 3/31.
• Multiple Foundever attendees - Antonino, Julio, Mariana, Andreina, David, Sadoc and Ana - joined the AI demo and asked detailed AI-related questions on multi-parameter AI negotiation, smart onboarding agent preview, and autonomous negotiation with market benchmarks. Strong cross-functional buyer engagement on AI-specific capabilities underpins the Hungry call.

3/18/26 MQ Foundever has issued an RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management and Onboarding. Responses are due by 3/31/26.

Foundever embarked in a Finance Transformation Project selecting SAP S4/Hana Public Cloud Edition for consolidating all ERP core Finance functionality across the Globe including the 42 Countries where Foundever has operations. The Implementation Project started January 2026 and will be executed in waves during next 2 years. First GoLive is January 2027 with 2 Countries and during 2027 and 2028 the remaining Countries to complete the 42 countries by the end of 2028.

As part of the scope, not all the P2P functionalities were considered and Foundever is exploring with potential recognized names on the P2P spectrum for completing those and selecting, ideally one partner, for covering all the missing functionalities. 6/2/26 MQ Per Julio they are still reviewing submissions for down selection to shortlist.


4/28/26 MQ Per Julio, Antonino is traveling this week. Suggested reaching out 5/7 for update.


4/8/26 MQ RFP response submitted 3/31. Antonino and Julio are doing initial review of submitted proposals, specifically evaluating how each aligns with their medium-to-long term strategy. I am offering to provide business case to support their efforts. Antonino expects to have update on next steps the week of 4/20.


3/18/26 MQ Foundever has issued an RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management and Onboarding. Responses are due by 3/31/26.

Foundever embarked in a Finance Transformation Project selecting SAP S4/Hana Public Cloud Edition for consolidating all ERP core Finance functionality across the Globe including the 42 Countries where Foundever has operations. The Implementation Project started January 2026 and will be executed in waves during next 2 years. First GoLive is January 2027 with 2 Countries and during 2027 and 2028 the remaining Countries to complete the 42 countries by the end of 2028.

As part of the scope, not all the P2P functionalities were considered and Foundever is exploring with potential recognized names on the P2P spectrum for completing those and selecting, ideally one partner, for covering all the missing functionalities. The intention is having an initial estimated cost of licensing and implementation by the end of March for presenting a Business Case to Executive Committee. Once approval obtained, refining proposals during April to June for awarding one or two Partners for covering the missing functionalities, starting the implementation during 2026 for GoLive January 2027 of the first 2 Countries.


3/5/26 MQ Foundever is standardizing/migrating all 42 sites to HANA/S4. Initial sites are US and Phillipines and are slated to go live 1/27. They will be using SAP for transactional P2P and have proposed to Coupa a 2yr. ramp down (Coupa is pushing back, renewal EOY). Looking to Zycus for CLM, eSourcing and Supplier Management with potential for additional footprint after Coupa ramp down. Wants us to go-live 1/27 on initial sites with other sites implemented concurrently w/ ERP roll out schedule. Want us to potentially replace existing Coupa P2P for 2 years if Coupa negotiations go south. Julio is sending 2yr. overall rollout scope.


3/2/26 MQ Scheduled meeting on 3/5/26 with Julio for update of project status.


2/19/26 MQ\xa0Working with Mariana and Julio to lock down date for group for Supplier Onboarding demonstration.


12/29/25\xa0MQ\xa0Followed up with Julio. He is OOO until 1/2/25.


12/3/25 MQ Held scheduled process timing/alignment meeting with Julio. He wants preliminary cost estimates for S2P suite vs. Supplier Management (onboarding) and eSourcing modules. Julio will gather metrics for pricing. He is checking schedules to see if it's feasible to schedule the follow up Supplier Management demo in December or wait until January.
